---
title: "Klima Protocol: Fee vs. Principal"
subtitle: "Revenue and risk for a carbon-retirement intermediary"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-location: right
---

```{=html}
<style>
  .pc-curve-box { border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px; background: #fafafa; padding: 0.75rem 1rem 1rem; margin: 0.75rem 0; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.4rem; margin: 0 0 0.6rem; align-items: center; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar button { padding: 0.3rem 0.7rem; border: 1px solid #888; background: #fff; color: #111; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 0.85rem; font-weight: 500; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar button:hover { background: #eef; border-color: #555; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar .hint { font-size: 0.82rem; color: #666; margin-left: auto; }
  .pc-curve-canvas { display: block; max-width: 100%; height: auto; touch-action: none; cursor: crosshair; background: #fff; border: 1px solid #ccc; border-radius: 6px; }
</style>
```

A carbon-retirement intermediary buys kVCM tokens and burns them on
behalf of clients. It can charge clients in one of two ways. A
**fee** is a markup on the token cost; it carries no inventory and
no price exposure. A **principal** price is a fixed USD quote per
tonne, with balance-sheet exposure to the spot price. Once the
principal book is open, the shape of its loss tail is a design
choice. The full derivations live on the [Model](model.qmd) page.

The intermediary retires $\lambda$ tonnes per unit time over a
horizon $T$ and sources $P$ kVCM per tonne at the spot price $S_t$
(in USD per kVCM). We write $N = \lambda T$ for the total tonnes
retired and $\Pi$ for a book's terminal P&L in USD. The fee and
back-to-back books share the same random driver: both are linear in
the spot integral $I_T = \int_0^T S_t \, dt$. At the break-even
quote $Q = Q^*$, the fixed USD-per-tonne quote that equalises the
two books' expected P&L $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$, the two means coincide.
The fee book enters with a positive sign and its revenue is
non-negative. The back-to-back book enters with a negative sign:
upside is capped at the quote payout $Q N$, and downside is
unbounded. Equal moments are not equal distributions.

A fully matched treasury pre-buys $k = N P$ kVCM at total USD basis
$C_{\mathrm{basis}} = N P S_0$, where $S_0$ is the spot at
inception. It cancels the $I_T$ term path by path: treasury
consumption contributes $+ P \lambda I_T$ against the back-to-back
book's $- P \lambda I_T$. The desk therefore collapses to the
deterministic $N(Q - P S_0)$. Partial coverage
$\alpha = \min(1, k / (N P))$ leaves the uncovered tail
$J_\alpha = \int_{\alpha T}^T S_t \, dt$. Quota-share syndication of
the back-to-back book at cession fraction $\beta \in [0, 1]$ scales
the variance by $(1 - \beta)^2$. At the actuarially fair premium
(counterparty risk load $\theta = 0$), the expected P&L of the
retained book $\mathbb{E}[\Pi_{\mathrm{ret}}]$ does not depend on
$\beta$.

Switching to fee pricing whenever $S_t \ge H = h S_0$ caps every
retirement done above the threshold at non-negative revenue. The
threshold multiple $h \ge 1$ controls how tight the cap is. The
switch is symmetric: re-entries below $H$ resume b2b exposure, so a
volatile path near $H$ can cross it several times. At the defaults
with $h = 1.25$, the 5 % tail-mean loss $\mathrm{CVaR}_{95}$ falls
well below the b2b reference and $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$ rises.
Tightening $h$ cuts the tail harder but invites more re-entries.
The full symbol table sits in [Setup and notation](model.qmd#setup).

---

## Specific scenarios

The paragraphs above average over every GBM future. Specific
scenarios, such as a mid-year crash or a historical replay, read
off a single realised path. The canvas below lets you draw one.
Each book's P&L on that drawn path is then recomputed and shown
against its Monte Carlo mean.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## How the drawn curve enters the books

A trapezoid rule over the drawn path yields $I_T$, the uncovered tail
$J_\alpha$, the switching split against $H = h S_0$, and the terminal
spot $S_T$. These feed every book on the [Simulator](simulator.qmd).
The GBM/Merton dynamics (drift $\mu$, diffusion $\sigma$, jumps
$\lambda_J, \mu_J, \sigma_J$) drive only the overlaid comparison
Monte Carlo, seeded by the PRNG slider.
:::

The buttons along the top of the canvas load common shapes. The
presets are a flat path at $S_0$, a linear drift of ±50 %, a
mid-horizon crash, a brief spike at $\tfrac13 T$, a random walk, and
a historical kVCM series read from `data/kvcm-historical.json` (the
file is populated by `npm run fetch:prices`). Clicking and dragging
on the canvas draws a freehand path that overrides the preset.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
historicalPrices = {
  // Fetched at build by `npm run fetch:prices` (Alchemy Prices API).
  try {
    const r = await fetch("data/kvcm-historical.json");
    if (!r.ok) return { data: [] };
    return await r.json();
  } catch (e) {
    return { data: [] };
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// X-axis anchor: prefer the fetchedAt timestamp from
// kvcm-historical.json; fall back to the current date. Captured once
// per page load.
buildDate = {
  const iso = historicalPrices && historicalPrices.fetchedAt;
  if (iso) {
    const parsed = new Date(iso);
    if (!isNaN(parsed.getTime())) return parsed;
  }
  return new Date();
}
```

::: {.side-panel-left .side-panel .pc-controls}
### Price process

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof pi0 = Inputs.range([0.1, 10000], { step: 0.01, value: 125, transform: Math.log, label: "π₀ (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof P = Inputs.range([1, 100000], { step: 0.01, value: 1400, transform: Math.log, label: "P (kVCM / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof mu = Inputs.range([-2, 2], { step: 0.005, value: -0.1, label: "drift μ (annualised)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sigma2 = Inputs.range([0, 10], {
  step: 0.001, value: 0.0625, label: "variance σ² (annualised)",
  transform: x => Math.log(x + 1e-3),
  invert: y => Math.max(0, Math.exp(y) - 1e-3),
})
```

### Jumps (Merton)

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof lambdaJ = Inputs.range([0, 100], {
  step: 0.01, value: 20, label: "jump intensity λ_J (/yr)",
  transform: x => Math.log(x + 1e-3),
  invert: y => Math.max(0, Math.exp(y) - 1e-3),
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof muJ = Inputs.range([-0.5, 0.5], { step: 0.01, value: -0.05, label: "mean log-jump μ_J" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sigmaJ = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.35, label: "log-jump SD σ_J" })
```

### Demand & inventory

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof lambdaDay = Inputs.range([0.001, 10000], { step: 0.001, value: 1, transform: Math.log, label: "retirements (tonnes / day)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof kPre = Inputs.range([0, 1000000], { step: 1000, value: 300000, label: "initial inventory (kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof cBasis = Inputs.range([0, 100000], { step: 100, value: 20000, label: "inventory basis (USD, total)" })
```

### Contract

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Step = 1 avoids a range-input snap when setting value to
// history.length - 1.
viewof Tdays = {
  const hist = (historicalPrices && Array.isArray(historicalPrices.data))
    ? historicalPrices.data
    : [];
  const fallback = 365;
  const dflt = hist.length >= 2
    ? Math.max(30, Math.min(1095, hist.length - 1))
    : fallback;
  return Inputs.range([30, 1095], { step: 1, value: dflt, label: "horizon T (days)" });
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof Q = Inputs.range([0.1, 10000], { step: 0.01, value: 175, transform: Math.log, label: "fixed quote Q (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof fee = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.4, label: "fee rate f" })
```

### Syndication

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof beta = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0, label: "cession β" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof theta = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0, label: "counterparty load θ" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof premiumMode = Inputs.radio(["sharpe", "cvar"], { value: "sharpe", label: "premium mode" })
```

### Switching

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof switchOn = Inputs.toggle({ label: "enable switching", value: false })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof barrierRatioSlider = Inputs.range([0, 10], {
  step: 0.01, value: 1.25, label: "threshold h = H/S₀",
  transform: x => Math.log(x + 1e-3),
  invert: y => Math.max(0, Math.exp(y) - 1e-3),
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Disabled switch ⇒ barrier Infinity.
barrierRatio = switchOn ? barrierRatioSlider : Infinity
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof feePostSlider = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.4, label: "fee-mode rate f_post" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
feePost = feePostSlider
```

### Curve settings

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveYMax = Inputs.range([0.01, 3], { step: 0.005, value: 0.2, label: "canvas y-max (USD / kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof seed = Inputs.range([1, 9999], { step: 1, value: 42, label: "PRNG seed" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
findingsResetButton = {
  const hist = (historicalPrices && Array.isArray(historicalPrices.data))
    ? historicalPrices.data
    : [];
  const defaultTdays = hist.length >= 2
    ? Math.max(30, Math.min(1095, hist.length - 1))
    : 365;
  const defaults = {
    pi0: 125, P: 1400,
    mu: -0.1, sigma2: 0.0625,
    lambdaJ: 20, muJ: -0.05, sigmaJ: 0.35,
    lambdaDay: 1, kPre: 300000, cBasis: 20000,
    Tdays: defaultTdays, Q: 175, fee: 0.4,
    beta: 0, theta: 0, premiumMode: "sharpe",
    switchOn: false, barrierRatioSlider: 1.25,
    feePostSlider: 0.4,
    curveYMax: 0.2, seed: 42,
  };
  const views = {
    pi0: viewof pi0, P: viewof P,
    mu: viewof mu, sigma2: viewof sigma2,
    lambdaJ: viewof lambdaJ, muJ: viewof muJ, sigmaJ: viewof sigmaJ,
    lambdaDay: viewof lambdaDay, kPre: viewof kPre, cBasis: viewof cBasis,
    Tdays: viewof Tdays, Q: viewof Q, fee: viewof fee,
    beta: viewof beta, theta: viewof theta, premiumMode: viewof premiumMode,
    switchOn: viewof switchOn, barrierRatioSlider: viewof barrierRatioSlider,
    feePostSlider: viewof feePostSlider,
    curveYMax: viewof curveYMax, seed: viewof seed,
  };
  const wrap = document.createElement("div");
  wrap.className = "pc-reset";
  const btn = document.createElement("button");
  btn.type = "button";
  btn.textContent = "Reset to defaults";
  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    for (const k of Object.keys(defaults)) {
      const el = views[k];
      el.value = defaults[k];
      el.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
    }
  });
  wrap.appendChild(btn);
  return wrap;
}
```

:::

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
import {
  mulberry32, xTicksAnchoredRight, formatTickDate, formatTickCurrency,
  simulate, simulateSwitching, summarise, samplePath,
} from "./lib/compiled/src/core/index.js"
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
derived = {
  const S0 = P > 0 ? pi0 / P : 0;
  const T = Tdays / 365;
  const lambda = lambdaDay * 365;
  const N = lambda * T;
  const sigma = Math.sqrt(sigma2);
  return { S0, T, lambda, N, sigma };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Comparison MC for the drawn-curve table. Params from sidebar;
// nPaths / nSteps pinned at Simulator defaults.
run = {
  const inputs = {
    S0: derived.S0, mu, sigma: derived.sigma, P,
    lambda: derived.lambda, T: derived.T,
    Q, fee, kPre, cBasis,
    beta, premiumLoad: theta, premiumMode,
    lambdaJ, muJ, sigmaJ,
    nPaths: 5000, nSteps: 100, seed,
  };
  const raw = simulate(inputs);
  const customDeskSamples = new Float64Array(raw.b2bSamples.length);
  for (let i = 0; i < customDeskSamples.length; i++) {
    customDeskSamples[i] = raw.b2bSamples[i] + raw.treasurySamples[i];
  }
  return {
    inputs,
    ...raw,
    customDeskSamples,
    fee: summarise(raw.feeSamples),
    retained: summarise(raw.retainedSamples),
    b2b: summarise(raw.b2bSamples),
    treasury: summarise(raw.treasurySamples),
    customDesk: summarise(customDeskSamples),
  };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Shares `seed` with `run`; switching + treasury composition deferred
// to Validation.
switchRun = {
  const inputs = {
    S0: derived.S0, mu, sigma: derived.sigma, P,
    lambda: derived.lambda, T: derived.T,
    Q, fee,
    barrierRatio, feePost,
    lambdaJ, muJ, sigmaJ,
    nPaths: 5000, nSteps: 100, seed,
  };
  const raw = simulateSwitching(inputs);
  return { inputs, ...raw, summary: summarise(raw.pnlSamples) };
}
```

::: {.collapsible-section}

### Price path

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof customCurve = {
  // One sample per day. Rightmost point = build-time anchor; leftmost
  // = Tdays ago.
  const TDaysVal = Tdays;
  const nPoints = TDaysVal + 1;
  const width = 820;
  const height = 320;
  const margin = { top: 10, right: 12, bottom: 32, left: 80 };
  const plotW = width - margin.left - margin.right;
  const plotH = height - margin.top - margin.bottom;
  const yMax = curveYMax;
  const S0Val = derived.S0;
  const history = (historicalPrices && Array.isArray(historicalPrices.data))
    ? historicalPrices.data
    : [];
  const seedVal = seed;
  const muVal = mu;
  const lambdaJVal = lambdaJ;
  const muJVal = muJ;
  const sigmaJVal = sigmaJ;

  // Persist drawn curve by days-ago (0 = build-time anchor). NaN =
  // "never drawn" so π₀/P changes refresh the fill via derived S₀.
  const MAX_TDAYS = 1095;
  const masterKey = "__findingsCustomCurveByDaysAgo";
  let master = window[masterKey];
  if (!(master instanceof Float64Array) || master.length !== MAX_TDAYS + 1) {
    master = new Float64Array(MAX_TDAYS + 1);
    master.fill(NaN);
    window[masterKey] = master;
  }

  // "random": slider nudges regenerate path + cloud. Otherwise the
  // curve stays put when jump sliders move. Persisted in `window` so
  // the flag survives cell re-runs.
  const modeKey = "__findingsCustomCurveMode";
  if (typeof window[modeKey] !== "string") window[modeKey] = "custom";

  // Seed the canvas with the historical series on first load.
  const seededKey = "__findingsCustomCurveSeeded";
  if (!window[seededKey]) {
    window[seededKey] = true;
    if (history.length >= 2) {
      const fillCount = Math.min(history.length, MAX_TDAYS + 1);
      for (let daysAgo = 0; daysAgo < fillCount; daysAgo++) {
        master[daysAgo] = history[history.length - 1 - daysAgo].value;
      }
    }
  }

  const values = new Float64Array(nPoints);
  for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
    const daysAgo = nPoints - 1 - i;
    const stored = master[daysAgo];
    values[i] = isNaN(stored) ? S0Val : stored;
  }

  // Mirrors simulator's mulberry32(seed) + samplePath; same
  // (seed, μ, σ, λ_J, μ_J, σ_J, T, S₀) yield identical clouds on
  // both pages. Path 0 is promoted to the drawn curve by "Random".
  const N_CLOUD = 20;
  const cloudRng = mulberry32(seedVal);
  const cloud = new Array(N_CLOUD);
  for (let j = 0; j < N_CLOUD; j++) {
    const { S } = samplePath(cloudRng, {
      S0: S0Val,
      mu: muVal,
      sigma: derived.sigma,
      T: derived.T,
      nSteps: nPoints - 1,
      lambdaJ: lambdaJVal, muJ: muJVal, sigmaJ: sigmaJVal,
    });
    cloud[j] = S;
  }

  if (window[modeKey] === "random") {
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) values[i] = cloud[0][i];
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) master[nPoints - 1 - i] = values[i];
  }

  const container = document.createElement("div");
  container.className = "pc-curve-box";

  const toolbar = document.createElement("div");
  toolbar.className = "pc-curve-toolbar";
  const hint = document.createElement("span");
  hint.className = "hint";
  hint.textContent = "Click and drag on the canvas to draw a price path.";

  const canvas = document.createElement("canvas");
  canvas.className = "pc-curve-canvas";
  canvas.width = width;
  canvas.height = height;
  const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");

  const indexToX = (i) => margin.left + (i / (nPoints - 1)) * plotW;
  const priceToY = (p) => margin.top + (1 - p / yMax) * plotH;
  const xToIndex = (x) =>
    Math.max(0, Math.min(nPoints - 1, Math.round(((x - margin.left) / plotW) * (nPoints - 1))));
  const yToPrice = (y) =>
    Math.max(0, Math.min(yMax, yMax - ((y - margin.top) / plotH) * yMax));

  const DAY_MS = 86400000;
  const anchor = buildDate;
  const dateForDay = (d) => new Date(anchor.getTime() - (TDaysVal - d) * DAY_MS);
  const xTicks = xTicksAnchoredRight(TDaysVal);
  const showCloud = lambdaJVal > 0 || derived.sigma > 0;
  function redraw() {
    ctx.clearRect(0, 0, width, height);
    ctx.strokeStyle = "#e5e5e5";
    ctx.lineWidth = 1;
    ctx.beginPath();
    for (let g = 1; g < 5; g++) {
      const y = margin.top + (g * plotH) / 5;
      ctx.moveTo(margin.left, y);
      ctx.lineTo(margin.left + plotW, y);
    }
    for (const d of xTicks) {
      if (d <= 0 || d >= TDaysVal) continue;
      const x = margin.left + (d / TDaysVal) * plotW;
      ctx.moveTo(x, margin.top);
      ctx.lineTo(x, margin.top + plotH);
    }
    ctx.stroke();

    ctx.strokeStyle = "#333";
    ctx.strokeRect(margin.left, margin.top, plotW, plotH);

    ctx.fillStyle = "#333";
    ctx.font = "11px ui-monospace, monospace";
    ctx.textAlign = "right";
    ctx.textBaseline = "middle";
    for (let g = 0; g <= 5; g++) {
      const priceVal = yMax * (1 - g / 5);
      const y = margin.top + (g * plotH) / 5;
      ctx.fillText("$" + priceVal.toFixed(3), margin.left - 6, y);
    }
    ctx.textBaseline = "top";
    for (const d of xTicks) {
      const x = margin.left + (d / TDaysVal) * plotW;
      const isRight = d === TDaysVal;
      const isLeft = d === 0;
      ctx.textAlign = isRight ? "right" : isLeft ? "left" : "center";
      ctx.fillText(formatTickDate(dateForDay(d), TDaysVal), x, margin.top + plotH + 6);
    }

    ctx.font = "12px sans-serif";
    ctx.save();
    ctx.translate(14, margin.top + plotH / 2);
    ctx.rotate(-Math.PI / 2);
    ctx.textAlign = "center";
    ctx.fillText("S_t (USD / kVCM)", 0, 0);
    ctx.restore();
    ctx.textAlign = "center";
    ctx.fillText("date", margin.left + plotW / 2, height - 2);

    if (S0Val > 0 && S0Val <= yMax) {
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#2a7";
      ctx.setLineDash([4, 3]);
      ctx.lineWidth = 1;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.moveTo(margin.left, priceToY(S0Val));
      ctx.lineTo(margin.left + plotW, priceToY(S0Val));
      ctx.stroke();
      ctx.setLineDash([]);
      ctx.fillStyle = "#2a7";
      ctx.textAlign = "left";
      ctx.textBaseline = "bottom";
      ctx.font = "11px ui-monospace, monospace";
      ctx.fillText("S₀", margin.left + 4, priceToY(S0Val) - 2);
    }

    if (showCloud) {
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#888";
      ctx.globalAlpha = 0.35;
      ctx.lineWidth = 1;
      ctx.lineJoin = "round";
      for (let j = 0; j < cloud.length; j++) {
        const S = cloud[j];
        ctx.beginPath();
        ctx.moveTo(indexToX(0), priceToY(Math.min(yMax, Math.max(0, S[0]))));
        for (let i = 1; i < nPoints; i++) {
          ctx.lineTo(indexToX(i), priceToY(Math.min(yMax, Math.max(0, S[i]))));
        }
        ctx.stroke();
      }
      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
    }

    ctx.strokeStyle = "#c94";
    ctx.lineWidth = 2;
    ctx.lineJoin = "round";
    ctx.beginPath();
    ctx.moveTo(indexToX(0), priceToY(values[0]));
    for (let i = 1; i < nPoints; i++) ctx.lineTo(indexToX(i), priceToY(values[i]));
    ctx.stroke();
  }

  function emit() {
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
      master[nPoints - 1 - i] = values[i];
    }
    container.value = {
      values: Float64Array.from(values),
      nPoints,
    };
    container.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
  }

  let lastIdx = null;
  let drawing = false;

  function apply(ev) {
    const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
    const x = ((ev.clientX - rect.left) * canvas.width) / rect.width;
    const y = ((ev.clientY - rect.top) * canvas.height) / rect.height;
    const idx = xToIndex(x);
    const price = yToPrice(y);
    if (lastIdx === null || lastIdx === idx) {
      values[idx] = price;
    } else {
      const lo = Math.min(idx, lastIdx);
      const hi = Math.max(idx, lastIdx);
      const loP = lastIdx <= idx ? values[lastIdx] : price;
      const hiP = lastIdx <= idx ? price : values[lastIdx];
      for (let k = lo; k <= hi; k++) {
        const frac = (k - lo) / (hi - lo);
        values[k] = loP * (1 - frac) + hiP * frac;
      }
    }
    lastIdx = idx;
  }

  canvas.addEventListener("pointerdown", (ev) => {
    window[modeKey] = "custom";
    ev.preventDefault();
    drawing = true;
    lastIdx = null;
    canvas.setPointerCapture?.(ev.pointerId);
    apply(ev);
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  canvas.addEventListener("pointermove", (ev) => {
    if (!drawing) return;
    ev.preventDefault();
    apply(ev);
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  const endStroke = (ev) => {
    drawing = false;
    lastIdx = null;
    if (canvas.hasPointerCapture?.(ev.pointerId)) canvas.releasePointerCapture(ev.pointerId);
  };
  canvas.addEventListener("pointerup", endStroke);
  canvas.addEventListener("pointercancel", endStroke);

  function setPreset(fn) {
    window[modeKey] = "custom";
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) values[i] = fn(i / (nPoints - 1));
    redraw();
    emit();
  }
  function addButton(label, fn) {
    const b = document.createElement("button");
    b.type = "button";
    b.textContent = label;
    b.addEventListener("click", fn);
    toolbar.appendChild(b);
  }
  addButton("Flat @ S₀", () => setPreset(() => S0Val));
  addButton("Linear +50%", () => setPreset((u) => S0Val * (1 + 0.5 * u)));
  addButton("Linear −50%", () => setPreset((u) => S0Val * (1 - 0.5 * u)));
  addButton("Crash @ ½T", () =>
    setPreset((u) => (u < 0.5 ? S0Val : S0Val * Math.max(0.05, 1 - 1.6 * (u - 0.5))))
  );
  addButton("Spike @ ⅓T", () => {
    const peak = S0Val * 2.5;
    setPreset((u) => {
      const d = u - 1 / 3;
      return S0Val + (peak - S0Val) * Math.exp(-80 * d * d);
    });
  });
  addButton("Random", () => {
    // Set mode before seed dispatch so the re-run reads modeKey at init.
    window[modeKey] = "random";
    const newSeed = 1 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 9999);
    const seedView = viewof seed;
    seedView.value = newSeed;
    seedView.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
  });
  const historicalBtn = document.createElement("button");
  historicalBtn.type = "button";
  historicalBtn.textContent = history.length >= 2 ? "Historical" : "Historical (unavailable)";
  historicalBtn.disabled = history.length < 2;
  historicalBtn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    // Flag before Tdays dispatch; right-align the daily series.
    window[modeKey] = "custom";
    const dayCount = history.length;
    const targetTdays = Math.max(30, Math.min(MAX_TDAYS, dayCount - 1));
    const tdaysView = viewof Tdays;

    if (Number(tdaysView.value) !== targetTdays) {
      // Stash into master before triggering the Tdays re-run.
      for (let daysAgo = 0; daysAgo <= targetTdays; daysAgo++) {
        master[daysAgo] = history[dayCount - 1 - daysAgo].value;
      }
      tdaysView.value = targetTdays;
      tdaysView.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
      return;
    }

    // Re-click at current Tdays: redraw in place.
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
      const daysAgo = nPoints - 1 - i;
      values[i] = history[dayCount - 1 - daysAgo].value;
    }
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  toolbar.appendChild(historicalBtn);
  toolbar.appendChild(hint);

  const legend = Plot.legend({
    color: {
      type: "ordinal",
      domain: [
        "drawn",
        ...(showCloud ? ["GBM samples"] : []),
        "S₀",
      ],
      range: [
        "#c94",
        ...(showCloud ? ["#888"] : []),
        "#2a7",
      ],
    },
  });

  container.appendChild(toolbar);
  container.appendChild(legend);
  container.appendChild(canvas);

  redraw();
  container.value = {
    values: Float64Array.from(values),
    nPoints,
  };
  return container;
}
```

:::

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
customScenario = {
  const { values: base, nPoints } = customCurve;
  const T = derived.T;
  const lam = derived.lambda;
  const N = derived.N;
  const dt = T / (nPoints - 1);

  let IT = 0;
  for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) {
    const w = (k === 0 || k === nPoints - 1) ? 0.5 : 1;
    IT += w * base[k];
  }
  IT *= dt;

  const tauFrac = (lam > 0 && P > 0)
    ? Math.min(1, kPre / (lam * P * T))
    : 1;
  const tailStartRaw = Math.ceil(tauFrac * (nPoints - 1));
  const tailStartIdx = tailStartRaw >= nPoints - 1 ? nPoints : tailStartRaw;
  let tailInt = 0;
  for (let k = tailStartIdx; k < nPoints; k++) {
    const w = (k === tailStartIdx || k === nPoints - 1) ? 0.5 : 1;
    tailInt += w * base[k];
  }
  tailInt *= dt;
  const consumptionInt = IT - tailInt;

  const ST = base[nPoints - 1];
  const tokensLeftover = Math.max(0, kPre - lam * T * P);
  const feeRev = fee * P * lam * IT;
  const b2bOp = Q * N - P * lam * IT;
  const treasury = P * lam * consumptionInt + tokensLeftover * ST - cBasis;
  const customDesk = b2bOp + treasury;

  const retainedOp = (1 - beta) * b2bOp + run.premium;

  const H = barrierRatio * derived.S0;
  let everCrossed = base[0] >= H;
  let crossed = everCrossed;
  let firstCrossTime = everCrossed ? 0 : T;
  let Ib2b = 0;
  let Ifee = 0;
  let tB2b = 0;
  let tFee = 0;
  for (let k = 1; k < nPoints; k++) {
    const sprev = base[k - 1];
    const piece = 0.5 * (sprev + base[k]) * dt;
    const prevAbove = sprev >= H;
    if (prevAbove) { Ifee += piece; tFee += dt; }
    else           { Ib2b += piece; tB2b += dt; }
    const currAbove = base[k] >= H;
    if (currAbove && !everCrossed) {
      firstCrossTime = k * dt;
      everCrossed = true;
      crossed = true;
    }
  }
  const fPost = feePost ?? fee;
  const switchingOp = Q * lam * tB2b - P * lam * Ib2b + fPost * P * lam * Ifee;

  let maxS = -Infinity, minS = Infinity;
  for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) {
    if (base[k] > maxS) maxS = base[k];
    if (base[k] < minS) minS = base[k];
  }
  const meanS = IT / T;

  return {
    base, nPoints, IT, tailInt, ST, tauFrac, tokensLeftover,
    feeRev, b2bOp, treasury, customDesk, retainedOp, switchingOp,
    crossed, firstCrossTime, tB2b, tFee, Ib2b, Ifee,
    maxS, minS, meanS,
  };
}
```

::: {.collapsible-section}

### Carbon price

The chart below shows the drawn path rescaled to carbon-price units
$\pi_t = S_t \cdot P$ (USD per tonne). The orange line is the drawn
curve, the grey lines are 20 GBM sample paths at the current
parameters, the dashed green rule marks the inventory-exhaustion
time $\alpha T$, and, when switching is on, the dashed red rule
marks the fee-mode threshold $\pi_H = h S_0 P$. The drawn curve is
the common input to all books below; the grey cloud shows where
GBM alone would have taken the price.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
carbonPriceRecords = {
  const { values, nPoints } = customCurve;
  const hasCloud = (lambdaJ > 0) || (derived.sigma > 0);
  const dtDays = Tdays / (nPoints - 1);
  const toTonne = (s) => s * P;

  const baseRows = new Array(nPoints);
  for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
    baseRows[i] = { tDays: i * dtDays, price: toTonne(values[i]), series: "drawn (rescaled by P)" };
  }

  const cloudRows = [];
  if (hasCloud) {
    const N_CLOUD = 20;
    const cloudRng = mulberry32(seed);
    for (let j = 0; j < N_CLOUD; j++) {
      const { S } = samplePath(cloudRng, {
        S0: derived.S0,
        mu,
        sigma: derived.sigma,
        T: derived.T,
        nSteps: nPoints - 1,
        lambdaJ, muJ, sigmaJ,
      });
      const id = "cloud-" + j;
      for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
        cloudRows.push({ tDays: i * dtDays, price: toTonne(S[i]), id, series: "GBM samples" });
      }
    }
  }

  return { baseRows, cloudRows, hasCloud };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 820,
  height: 320,
  marginLeft: 70,
  marginBottom: 50,
  grid: true,
  x: {
    label: "date",
    domain: [0, Tdays],
    ticks: xTicksAnchoredRight(Tdays),
    tickFormat: d => formatTickDate(
      new Date(buildDate.getTime() - (Tdays - d) * 86400000), Tdays),
  },
  y: { label: "carbon price (USD / tonne)", zero: true, tickFormat: formatTickCurrency },
  color: {
    legend: true,
    domain: [
      "drawn (rescaled by P)",
      ...(carbonPriceRecords.hasCloud ? ["GBM samples"] : []),
      "αT",
      ...(switchOn ? ["fee-mode threshold π_H (fee above)"] : []),
    ],
    range: [
      "#c94",
      ...(carbonPriceRecords.hasCloud ? ["#888"] : []),
      "#2a7",
      ...(switchOn ? ["#a36"] : []),
    ],
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    ...(carbonPriceRecords.hasCloud
      ? [Plot.line(carbonPriceRecords.cloudRows,
          { x: "tDays", y: "price", z: "id", stroke: "series", strokeOpacity: 0.25 })]
      : []),
    Plot.line(carbonPriceRecords.baseRows,
      { x: "tDays", y: "price", stroke: "series", strokeWidth: 2 }),
    Plot.ruleX([{ t: run.tauFrac * Tdays, kind: "αT" }],
      { x: "t", stroke: "kind", strokeDasharray: "4 3", strokeWidth: 2 }),
    ...(switchOn
      ? [Plot.ruleY([{ y: barrierRatio * derived.S0 * P, kind: "fee-mode threshold π_H (fee above)" }],
          { y: "y", stroke: "kind", strokeDasharray: "6 3", strokeWidth: 2 })]
      : []),
  ],
})
```

:::

::: {.collapsible-section}

### P&L along the path

The chart below plots cumulative P&L per book, in USD, against
calendar date. Each coloured line is one book evaluated on the
drawn path up to that date; the dashed green rule marks the
inventory-exhaustion time $\alpha T$. The custom desk kinks at
$\alpha T$ when inventory runs out, and the switching book kinks
at each crossing of $H$.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
pnlOverTime = {
  const { values: realised, nPoints } = customCurve;
  const T = derived.T;
  const lam = derived.lambda;
  const dt = T / (nPoints - 1);
  const dtDays = Tdays / (nPoints - 1);
  const N = derived.N;

  // Same αT breakpoint as customScenario so the terminal desk matches.
  const tauFrac = (lam > 0 && P > 0)
    ? Math.min(1, kPre / (lam * P * T))
    : 1;
  const tailStartRaw = Math.ceil(tauFrac * (nPoints - 1));
  const tailStartIdx = tailStartRaw >= nPoints - 1 ? nPoints : tailStartRaw;

  const H = barrierRatio * derived.S0;
  const includeSwitch = switchOn;
  const switchLabel = `switching (h=${barrierRatio.toFixed(2)})`;
  const fPost = feePost ?? fee;

  let I = 0;
  let Ib2b = 0;
  let Ifee = 0;
  let tB2b = 0;
  let tFee = 0;

  const treasuryOpen = kPre * derived.S0 - cBasis;

  const rows = [];
  for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) {
    if (k > 0) {
      const sprev = realised[k - 1];
      const piece = 0.5 * (sprev + realised[k]) * dt;
      I += piece;
      if (sprev >= H) { Ifee += piece; tFee += dt; }
      else            { Ib2b += piece; tB2b += dt; }
    }

    // Edge-weighted tail integral on [tailStartIdx, k]; O(nPoints²)
    // but nPoints ≤ 1096.
    let tailIk = 0;
    if (k > tailStartIdx && tailStartIdx < nPoints) {
      for (let j = tailStartIdx; j <= k; j++) {
        const w = (j === tailStartIdx || j === k) ? 0.5 : 1;
        tailIk += w * realised[j];
      }
      tailIk *= dt;
    }

    const tK = k * dt;
    const heldTokens = Math.max(0, kPre - lam * P * tK);
    const consumptionIk = I - tailIk;
    const feePnl = fee * P * lam * I;
    const b2bPnl = Q * lam * tK - P * lam * I;
    const retainedPnl = (1 - beta) * b2bPnl + run.premium * (tK / T);
    const treasuryPnl = P * lam * consumptionIk + heldTokens * realised[k] - cBasis;
    const customDeskPnl = b2bPnl + treasuryPnl;

    const tDays = k * dtDays;
    rows.push({ tDays, book: "fee", pnl: feePnl });
    rows.push({ tDays, book: "b2b", pnl: b2bPnl });
    rows.push({ tDays, book: "retained (syndicated-on-b2b)", pnl: retainedPnl });
    rows.push({ tDays, book: "treasury", pnl: treasuryPnl });
    rows.push({ tDays, book: "custom desk = b2b + treasury", pnl: customDeskPnl });
    if (includeSwitch) {
      const switchPnl = Q * lam * tB2b - P * lam * Ib2b + fPost * P * lam * Ifee;
      rows.push({ tDays, book: switchLabel, pnl: switchPnl });
    }
  }

  return { rows, includeSwitch, switchLabel, treasuryOpen };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 820,
  height: 360,
  marginLeft: 80,
  marginBottom: 50,
  grid: true,
  x: {
    label: "date",
    domain: [0, Tdays],
    ticks: xTicksAnchoredRight(Tdays),
    tickFormat: d => formatTickDate(
      new Date(buildDate.getTime() - (Tdays - d) * 86400000), Tdays),
  },
  y: { label: "partial P&L (USD)", tickFormat: formatTickCurrency },
  color: {
    legend: true,
    domain: [
      "fee",
      "b2b",
      "retained (syndicated-on-b2b)",
      "treasury",
      "custom desk = b2b + treasury",
      ...(pnlOverTime.includeSwitch ? [pnlOverTime.switchLabel] : []),
      "αT",
    ],
    range: [
      "#4e79a7",
      "#f28e2c",
      "#e15759",
      "#76b7b2",
      "#59a14f",
      ...(pnlOverTime.includeSwitch ? ["#edc949"] : []),
      "#2a7",
    ],
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.ruleX([{ t: run.tauFrac * Tdays, kind: "αT" }],
      { x: "t", stroke: "kind", strokeDasharray: "4 3", strokeWidth: 2 }),
    Plot.line(pnlOverTime.rows,
      { x: "tDays", y: "pnl", stroke: "book", z: "book", strokeWidth: 1.75 }),
  ],
})
```

_Operating books start at 0; treasury opens at MTM $k S_0 -
C_{\mathrm{basis}}$. Switching slope alternates at each $H = h S_0$
crossing._

:::

::: {.collapsible-section}

### Terminal P&L

The table below reports one-point statistics for each book. The
`drawn` column is the book's P&L on the sketched path at time $T$.
The `E[Π] (MC)` column is the Monte Carlo mean over 5,000 GBM
sample paths at the current parameters. The `diff` column is the
former minus the latter.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="derived-stats">
  <div class="card"><div class="label">min / max drawn S</div><div class="value">${"$" + customScenario.minS.toFixed(4)} / ${"$" + customScenario.maxS.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">mean drawn S (I_T / T)</div><div class="value">${"$" + customScenario.meanS.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">terminal S_T</div><div class="value">${"$" + customScenario.ST.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">tail integral ∫_{αT}^{T} S_t dt</div><div class="value">${customScenario.tailInt.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
</div>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
customRows = [
  { book: "fee",                          pnl: customScenario.feeRev,     mcMean: run.fee.mean,        diff: customScenario.feeRev     - run.fee.mean },
  { book: "b2b",                          pnl: customScenario.b2bOp,      mcMean: run.b2b.mean,        diff: customScenario.b2bOp      - run.b2b.mean },
  { book: "retained (syndicated-on-b2b)", pnl: customScenario.retainedOp, mcMean: run.retained.mean,   diff: customScenario.retainedOp - run.retained.mean },
  { book: "treasury",                     pnl: customScenario.treasury,   mcMean: run.treasury.mean,   diff: customScenario.treasury   - run.treasury.mean },
  { book: "custom desk = b2b + treasury", pnl: customScenario.customDesk, mcMean: run.customDesk.mean, diff: customScenario.customDesk - run.customDesk.mean },
  ...(switchOn
    ? [{
        book: `switching (h=${barrierRatio.toFixed(2)})`,
        pnl: customScenario.switchingOp,
        mcMean: switchRun.summary.mean,
        diff: customScenario.switchingOp - switchRun.summary.mean,
      }]
    : []),
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(customRows, {
  columns: ["book", "pnl", "mcMean", "diff"],
  header: {
    book: "book",
    pnl: "P&L on drawn curve",
    mcMean: "E[Π] (MC)",
    diff: "drawn − MC mean",
  },
  format: {
    pnl: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    mcMean: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    diff: x => (x >= 0 ? "+$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
  },
})
```

_**drawn**: P&L on the sketched path. **MC mean**: $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$
at defaults ($\mu = -0.1$, $\sigma^2 = 0.0625$, $\lambda_J = 20$,
5,000 paths). **diff** = drawn − MC mean._

A negative `diff` on the `custom desk` row means the drawn token was
pricier over $[\alpha T, T]$ than GBM expects on average. At full
coverage the desk is path-insensitive and the `diff` collapses to
zero.

:::

```{=html}
<script>
(function () {
  for (const sec of document.querySelectorAll('.collapsible-section')) {
    const heading = sec.querySelector('h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6');
    if (!heading) continue;
    const details = document.createElement('details');
    details.open = true;
    details.className = 'collapsible-section';
    const summary = document.createElement('summary');
    summary.appendChild(heading);
    details.appendChild(summary);
    while (sec.firstChild) details.appendChild(sec.firstChild);
    sec.replaceWith(details);
  }
})();
</script>
```

## Scope

The model is a GBM baseline with optional Merton jumps and
deterministic demand; see [baselines this note assumes](model.qmd#baselines).
The outputs are illustrative, not forecasts.
